task_desc: Fix test runner silent failure swallowing and hardened test infrastructure -- The test runner (runAllTests in tests/tests.js) has a critical bug: it catches all TestFailure exceptions in a try/catch and writes results only to DOM elements via innerHTML. When run in Node.js (the only automated test environment), failed tests produce exit code 0 with zero console output, making automated regression detection completely non-functional. Confirmed by injecting a deliberately failing test: exits 0 silently. Three related issues to fix together: (1) Add a CLI-compatible output path to runAllTests: after the test loop, console.log the pass/fail/total counts and each failure message, then throw or set process.exitCode = 1 when failures > 0 so Node.js execution surfaces regressions; guard the console/process calls behind typeof checks so the browser path is unaffected, (2) The five functions copied verbatim from main.js into tests.js (normalizeAngle, BEHAVIOR_THRESHOLDS, isCoolingDown, hasNearbyFood, evaluateBehaviorEntry) are frozen snapshots that will silently diverge when main.js changes, causing tests to validate stale logic while the real code regresses. Refactor main.js to separate pure testable functions and constants from DOM-dependent initialization into a shared file (e.g. js/fly-logic.js loaded before main.js) that both main.js and tests.js can reference, eliminating the copy-paste drift risk, (3) Add test coverage for T5.1-T5.3 features that currently have zero tests: dark settling drive modulation (fatigue gain doubles to 0.006 at lightLevel < 0.3, curiosity range drops to 0.02, tonic injection halves to 4 at lightLevel 0), dark rest threshold (0.4 vs 0.7), temperature stimulus routing (THERMO_WARM fires at temperature > 0.65, THERMO_COOL at < 0.35), nociception single-tick auto-clear behavior, and brace entry conditions (wind active AND windStrength < 0.5 AND accumStartle below threshold). Also fix the misleading windDirection comment at connectome.js:142 which says "direction wind is blowing FROM" but the code computes and uses it as the wind travel direction (drag vector direction); the brace code at main.js:675 correctly treats windDirection + PI as the wind source direction, confirming the field comment is inverted. Files: tests/tests.js, tests/run.html, js/main.js, js/connectome.js.
